title: Meta, Google Found Liable in Social Media Addiction Case
snippet: A jury verdict found Meta and Alphabet's Google liable for harming a young user with products designed to be addictive. As Bloomberg's Annabelle Droulers reports, the decision may lead to increased government regulation…
contentText: A jury verdict found Meta and Alphabet's Google liable for harming a young user with products designed to be addictive. As Bloomberg's Annabelle Droulers reports, the decision may lead to increased government regulations on social networking companies. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The least surprising chapter of the Manus story is what’s happening right now
snippet: Did anyone think there would not be a reckoning over this tie-up?
contentText: Did anyone think there would not be a reckoning over this tie-up?

title: Google Breakthrough Spurs Chip Selloff Despite Analysts’ Doubt
snippet: Shares of computer memory and storage products slumped on concerns over demand after Google researchers touted a new compression technique. But it may be a hiccup rather than an existential threat.
contentText: Shares of computer memory and storage products slumped on concerns over demand after Google researchers touted a new compression technique. But it may be a hiccup rather than an existential threat.

title: Datacenter batteries are selling out years in advance, because AI, says Panasonic
snippet: Shifting production from automotive to compute and working on supercapacitors as another way to protect workloads Major memory makers have already sold all the kit they can make this year, creating shortages and price i…
contentText: Shifting production from automotive to compute and working on supercapacitors as another way to protect workloads Major memory makers have already sold all the kit they can make this year, creating shortages and price increases. Datacenter infrastructure buyers may soon face the same issues when trying to get their hands on backup batteries.…

title: Meta, Google Risk Big Tobacco-Like Fallout After Addiction Trial
snippet: A landmark jury verdict holding Meta Platforms Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google liable for harming a young user with products designed to be addictive threatens to put the social networking companies in the same category…
contentText: A landmark jury verdict holding Meta Platforms Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google liable for harming a young user with products designed to be addictive threatens to put the social networking companies in the same category as Big Tobacco and opioid makers — a potential crack in their shield from legal responsibility for what happens on their platforms.

title: Mercor competitor Deccan AI raises $25M, sources experts from India
snippet: Deccan AI concentrates its workforce in India to manage quality in a fast-growing but fragmented AI training market.
contentText: Deccan AI concentrates its workforce in India to manage quality in a fast-growing but fragmented AI training market.

title: Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and B65 Video Cards: Big Battlemage Brings Big Memory for AI Workstations
snippet: Today Intel is expanding their Arc B-series video card lineup in a big way, with the launch of a pair of new Arc Pro graphics cards: the Arc Pro B70 and the Arc Pro B65. Joining Intel’s existing Arc Pro B-series video c…
contentText: Today Intel is expanding their Arc B-series video card lineup in a big way, with the launch of a pair of new Arc Pro graphics cards: the Arc Pro B70 and the Arc Pro B65. Joining Intel’s existing Arc Pro B-series video cards, the latest cards out of Intel are also the company’s most powerful […] The post Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and B65 Video Cards: Big Battlemage Brings Big Memory for AI Workstations appeared first on ServeTheHome .

title: Meituan Hedges Get Costly as Price Wars Make Investors Cautious

contentText: Meituan’s stock price jump on Wednesday came with a rise in protection costs, highlighting investor unease ahead of earnings that may show a big hit from stiff price competition.

title: Truce Prospects Lift Stocks | The Close 3/25/2026
snippet: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Edward Jones’ Mona Mahajan, Lotus Technology…
contentText: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Edward Jones’ Mona Mahajan, Lotus Technology Management’s Alap Shah, Mizuho’s David Bellinger, S&P Global Energy President Dave Ernsberger, Wells Fargo Investment Institute’s Veronica Willis, BMO’s Mark McCormick, Willow Wealth CEO Mitchell Caplan, & Pleasant/Rock Co-Founders Brian K. Hinds Jr. & Malcolm Jenkins. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Prologis CEO Sees Prolonged AI, Data Center Tailwinds
snippet: Prologis CEO Dan Letter joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss the state of global logistics and shipping as geopolitics and associated risk impacts the firm's customer base. However, Prologis is optimistic about…
contentText: Prologis CEO Dan Letter joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss the state of global logistics and shipping as geopolitics and associated risk impacts the firm's customer base. However, Prologis is optimistic about its data center business. Letter says the company's strong relationship with hyperscalers is ongoing, bolstering "a fortress of a balance sheet" as Prologis continues to secure longer-term power capacity. Letter speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
snippet: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.
contentText: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.

title: Cairn Looks to Hire Americans to Build India’s Shale Sector
snippet: Vedanta Ltd.’s Cairn Oil & Gas is looking to quadruple daily output to the equivalent of 1 million barrels over the better part of the next decade as India’s largest private sector oil producers basks in the war-drive s…
contentText: Vedanta Ltd.’s Cairn Oil & Gas is looking to quadruple daily output to the equivalent of 1 million barrels over the better part of the next decade as India’s largest private sector oil producers basks in the war-drive surge in crude prices.

title: The EU’s Biggest Test for Device Makers: Replaceable Batteries
snippet: European Union rules requiring electronics to include easily replaceable batteries could force consumer tech makers to rethink their designs and go beyond the repairability improvements they’ve made in recent years.
contentText: European Union rules requiring electronics to include easily replaceable batteries could force consumer tech makers to rethink their designs and go beyond the repairability improvements they’ve made in recent years.

title: Google unveils TurboQuant, a new AI memory compression algorithm — and yes, the internet is calling it ‘Pied Piper’
snippet: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.
contentText: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.

title: Announcing Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL serverless database creation in seconds
snippet: AWS introduces a new express configuration for Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL, a streamlined database creation experience with preconfigured defaults designed to help you get started in seconds. With Aurora PostgreSQL, start…
contentText: At re:Invent 2025, Colin Lazier , vice president of databases at AWS, emphasized the importance of building at the speed of an idea—enabling rapid progress from concept to running application. Customers can already create production-ready Amazon DynamoDB tables and Amazon Aurora DSQL databases in seconds. He previewed creating an Amazon Aurora serverless database with the same speed, and customers have since requested quick access and speed to this capability. Today, we’re announcing the general availability of a new express configuration for Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL, a streamlined database creation experience with preconfigured defaults designed to help you get started in seconds. With only two clicks, you can have an Aurora PostgreSQL serverless database ready to use in seconds. You hav…

title: Mexico Bets on Supercomputer to Combat Extreme Weather Events
snippet: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum is working to develop climate models that will help prevent extreme weather events, part of a push to set up a public supercomputer that can use millions of data points to tackle nati…
contentText: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum is working to develop climate models that will help prevent extreme weather events, part of a push to set up a public supercomputer that can use millions of data points to tackle national challenges.

title: SpaceX Could File an IPO By End of the Week | Bloomberg Tech 3/25/2026
snippet: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss Arm’s plans to sell its own chips for the first time, with Meta as the first major customer. Plus, space stocks climb after reports that SpaceX aims to file a prospectus f…
contentText: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss Arm’s plans to sell its own chips for the first time, with Meta as the first major customer. Plus, space stocks climb after reports that SpaceX aims to file a prospectus for its mega IPO before the end of March. And, Kleiner Perkins partner Ilya Fushman discusses the firm’s largest raise ever to back AI bets. (Source: Bloomberg)

contentText: The European Commission’s proposed Digital Networks Act represents an important development for the digital infrastructure ecosystem, writes Vincent Garnier.

title: Only Trump can decide when cyberwar turns into real war
snippet: Four former NSA bosses walk onto the stage at RSAC… rsac 2026 There's a theoretical red line with cyber warfare. Cross it, and the US will respond with a physical attack like missile strikes. And that line "is whatever…
contentText: Four former NSA bosses walk onto the stage at RSAC… rsac 2026 There's a theoretical red line with cyber warfare. Cross it, and the US will respond with a physical attack like missile strikes. And that line "is whatever the President says it is," according to former NSA boss retired General Paul Nakasone.…

title: Advent's Malani Sees Gaps in Defense Tech Superiority
snippet: Shonnel Malani, Advent's global head of aerospace and defense, says the company plans to invest as much as $1 billion in defense tech because of increasing global geopolitical tensions. He speaks with Dani Burger on "Bl…
contentText: Shonnel Malani, Advent's global head of aerospace and defense, says the company plans to invest as much as $1 billion in defense tech because of increasing global geopolitical tensions. He speaks with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Kleiner Perkins Raises $3.5 Billion to Back AI Boom
snippet: Kleiner Perkins has raised $3.5 billion to make bets on AI startups and current portfolio companies innovating across autonomy, transportation, professional services, and space. Kleiner Perkins partner Ilya Fushman join…
contentText: Kleiner Perkins has raised $3.5 billion to make bets on AI startups and current portfolio companies innovating across autonomy, transportation, professional services, and space. Kleiner Perkins partner Ilya Fushman joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Google DeepMind, Agile Robots Team Up to Reshape AI Data Center Demand
snippet: As AI moves from training clusters into factories, warehouses, and autonomous systems, operators face rising pressure on edge infrastructure, data pipelines, and continuous retraining cycles.
contentText: As AI moves from training clusters into factories, warehouses, and autonomous systems, operators face rising pressure on edge infrastructure, data pipelines, and continuous retraining cycles.

title: AI Doomer’s New Warning: Market Is Wrong to Ditch Rate-Cut Bets
snippet: A little over a month after James van Geelen sent stocks into a tailspin with his dystopian account of a post-AI economic collapse, he’s wagering that another fear-fueled selloff — this time, in the bond market — has ru…
contentText: A little over a month after James van Geelen sent stocks into a tailspin with his dystopian account of a post-AI economic collapse, he’s wagering that another fear-fueled selloff — this time, in the bond market — has run too far.

title: Defense Tech Gets Less Than 1% of Pentagon Spending
snippet: Pentagon spending on defense tech from startups has more than doubled, from about $1.8 billion in Fiscal Year 2023 to $4.3 billion for Fiscal Year 2025, but still makes up less than 1% of total contract dollars. Rachel…
contentText: Pentagon spending on defense tech from startups has more than doubled, from about $1.8 billion in Fiscal Year 2023 to $4.3 billion for Fiscal Year 2025, but still makes up less than 1% of total contract dollars. Rachel Hoff, policy director at the Ronald Reagan Institute, speaks with Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Virginia Rep. Calls for Spreading AI Build-Out Across the Country
snippet: Senator Bernie Sanders and Congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez plan to introduce legislation that would impose a moratorium on new AI data centers, following growing pushback on their impact on energy prices. Represe…
contentText: Senator Bernie Sanders and Congresswoman Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez plan to introduce legislation that would impose a moratorium on new AI data centers, following growing pushback on their impact on energy prices. Representative Suhas Subramanyam, a Democrat from Virginia, home to one of the largest concentrations of data centers in the country, discusses what approach he thinks the country should take to the AI build-out. He speaks with Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why AI Won’t Be the End of Hollywood
snippet: Disruption hurts. But Bloomberg Opinion columnist Parmy Olson has reason for cautious optimism about AI and Hollywood. (Source: Bloomberg)
contentText: Disruption hurts. But Bloomberg Opinion columnist Parmy Olson has reason for cautious optimism about AI and Hollywood. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Geekbench 6 warns about inconsistent benchmarking performance from new Core Ultra 200S Plus chips — says Intel's IPC boosting Binary Optimization Tool modifies scores in 'unclear' fashion
snippet: The team behind Geekbench 6 has warned users about benchmarking inconsistency with Intel's latest iBOT tool found in the new Core Ultra 7 270K Plus and Ultra 5 250K Plus. Geekbench 6 can't identify when iBOT is enabled…
contentText: The team behind Geekbench 6 has warned users about benchmarking inconsistency with Intel's latest iBOT tool found in the new Core Ultra 7 270K Plus and Ultra 5 250K Plus. Geekbench 6 can't identify when iBOT is enabled or disabled during benchmark runs.

title: Meta, Google Found Liable in First Social Media Addiction Trial
snippet: Meta Platforms Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google must pay damages to a 20-year-old woman who said her addiction to social media caused her mental health struggles, a jury concluded in a landmark decision that could signal…
contentText: Meta Platforms Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google must pay damages to a 20-year-old woman who said her addiction to social media caused her mental health struggles, a jury concluded in a landmark decision that could signal hefty risks for the companies as they fight thousands of similar claims.

title: Musk’s xAI ‘Doubling Down’ on AI Videos After OpenAI Nixes Sora
snippet: Elon Musk said his artificial intelligence company, xAI, plans to upgrade its AI video generator, seizing on an opening left by rival OpenAI’s decision this week to discontinue its Sora product.
contentText: Elon Musk said his artificial intelligence company, xAI, plans to upgrade its AI video generator, seizing on an opening left by rival OpenAI’s decision this week to discontinue its Sora product.
